### Post by skaboss on 2008-08-10
Hello,

I have a usb hard drive (running ntfs file system), which just worked perfectly until last week...
When i plug it into my computer, it mounts and unmounts on its own several times, then i can browse my drive without problem, but just can't write to it. Quite annoying for a storage disk...
Can someone help me please ?

Here is my fstab and mtab :

fstab :
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=69e19288-9b52-41cf-9a3d-3729c3052bbe / ext3 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/ !! UNKNOW DEVICE !! :
UUID=7a105f0a-8061-49d4-a671-e34f3c63ca4f none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hda /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
/dev/sda1 /media/PQSERVICE ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0

```
Seems like ntfs-config messed up something...

Here is mtab : 

```

/dev/sda5 / ext3 rw,relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 0
proc /proc proc rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev 0 0
/sys /sys sysfs rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev 0 0
varrun /var/run tmpfs rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=0755 0 0
varlock /var/lock tmpfs rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=1777 0 0
udev /dev tmpfs rw,mode=0755 0 0
devshm /dev/shm tmpfs rw 0 0
devpts /dev/pts devpts rw,gid=5,mode=620 0 0
lrm /lib/modules/2.6.24-17-generic/volatile tmpfs rw 0 0
/dev/sda1 /media/PQSERVICE fuseblk rw,nosuid,nodev,noatime,allow_other,blksize=4096 0 0
securityfs /sys/kernel/security securityfs rw 0 0
binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc binfmt_misc rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev 0 0
gvfs-fuse-daemon /home/landry/.gvfs fuse.gvfs-fuse-daemon rw,nosuid,nodev,user=landry 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /media/FreeAgent\040Drive fuseblk rw,nosuid,nodev,noatime,allow_other,blksize=4096 0 0

```

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-08-10
Can you post the output of:-
```
sudo fdisk -l
```
and point out the hard disk in the output.

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-08-10
From the error and the fdisk -l output, I think your hard drive may have had it. What filesystem is the hard drive using?

---

### Post by skaboss on 2008-08-10
It's formatted in ntfs, and works correctly with my other (XP) computer.

---

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-08-10
> **skaboss said:**
> It's formatted in ntfs, and works correctly with my other (XP) computer.

Then run ntfsfix on the drive, install ntfsfix with:-
```
sudo apt-get install ntfsprogs
```
and then run it on the hard drive with:-
```
sudo ntfsfix /dev/sdb
```
post any errors you receive here.

---

### Post by skaboss on 2008-08-10
Actually, this drive contains dozens gigabytes of files that i wouldn't like to lose, so i'm quite reluctant to use ntfsfix. 
I tend to think my problem is Hardy related, since this usb disk runs just fine with both my xp and my Gutsy box...
